summary: Similar triangles have the same shape (equal corresponding angles) and proportional corresponding sides.
explanation: Two triangles are similar if their corresponding angles are equal, which forces their corresponding sides to be in the same fixed ratio. A common way similar triangles appear is when a smaller triangle is cut off inside a larger one by a line parallel to one side — this always creates two similar triangles. To solve, identify which sides correspond (matching up angles carefully), set up a proportion, and cross-multiply. A frequent trap: the ratio of AREAS between similar figures is the SQUARE of the ratio of corresponding sides, not the same as the side ratio — if triangle sides are in a 2:3 ratio, the areas are in a 4:9 ratio, not 2:3.
